source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Direct evidence that hippocampal sequences can be biased toward remembered goals before action. This strongly supports a goal-guided probe policy, even though the exact SWR vs theta carrier question remains open.
failure_signatures: If hippocampal future sweeps were insensitive to remembered goals, ghost-goal-guided waking probes would have weak biological support.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal population sequences can depict paths toward remembered goals before navigation.
mapping.ree_translation: A waking proposal branch seeded by remembered unresolved goals is biologically plausible because hippocampal search already supports goal-biased future sweeps.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper does not test blocked or deferred goals specifically.
mapping.source_context: Rat CA1 ensemble decoding during goal-directed navigation.

confidence_components.notes: The key support is for goal-directed future-path generation, not the exact rhythm or storage format.
